entries.title: Exposition on Psalm 118
entries.excerpt: ...We are taught in this Psalm, when we chaunt Allelujah, which means, Praise the Lord, that we should, when we hear the words, Confess unto the Lord Psalm 117:1, praise the Lord. The praise of God could not be expressed in fewer words than these, For He is good. I see not what can be more solemn than this brevity, since goodness is so peculiarly the quality of God, that the Son of God Himself when addressed by some one as Good Master, by one, namely, who beholding His flesh, and comprehending not the fullness of His divine nature, considered Him as man only, replied, Why do you call Me good?…
